summary: iteration_2 — regression + new LIGHT/DARK theme toggle feature. Backend: all 10 pytest cases in test_workquire_api.py PASS (health email_configured:true, VA + Team inquiry POSTs return email_sent:true via Resend, validation 422s, GET list sorted desc, no MongoDB _id leak). Frontend: full theme toggle suite validated at 1920x1080 and 320x700. Default theme is LIGHT on fresh visit (html has no .dark class, body bg = rgb(234,244,255)). Clicking theme-toggle toggles html.dark class, persists wq-theme in localStorage, and updates body bg to rgb(11,17,32) in dark mode — both matching spec exactly. Theme persists across reload. 3D Three.js canvas remains mounted inside hero-3d-canvas-wrapper after palette swap in both themes. All 7 key sections render in light mode. VA dialog (no team_size) and Team dialog (with team_size) both submit successfully in light mode and show inquiry-success. Mobile 320px: theme-toggle is directly visible in the navbar (not hidden behind hamburger), mobile-inquire-va-btn & mobile-inquire-team-btn show after opening nav-mobile-toggle, theme toggling works on mobile, no horizontal overflow.
critical_code_review_comments: Console warning (non-blocking): 'THREE.Clock: This module has been deprecated. Please use THREE.Timer instead.' emits on every HeroBackground mount. Since the scene rebuilds on theme change (dep:[theme]), the deprecation warning is logged twice per toggle. Consider switching to THREE.Timer in HeroBackground.jsx to silence the warning and future-proof against three.js removing Clock.

Carryover from iteration_1 (still applicable): @app.on_event('shutdown') in backend/server.py is deprecated — migrate to FastAPI lifespan handlers. Resend SENDER_EMAIL is still onboarding@resend.dev (test sender) — swap to a verified domain before production.

Theme bootstrap is currently done inside a useEffect inside ThemeProvider, which means there's a one-frame window where html has no .dark class even when stored theme is 'dark'. On a fast reload this produces a brief light-mode FOUC before dark theme is applied. Consider injecting a tiny inline pre-React script in public/index.html that reads localStorage 'wq-theme' and sets html.classList.add('dark') synchronously to eliminate the flash.

ThemeToggle.jsx uses hardcoded hex colors (#dceaff, rgba(0,40,90,0.18), etc.) instead of CSS variables. Functionally fine, but if the palette token system is later extended (e.g., a third theme), these would need manual updates. Low priority.
test_credentials: N/A — public landing page, no auth.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests + frontend dialog submissions create a handful of TEST_-prefixed inquiries in MongoDB during the run. Not cleaned up (small payload, acceptable).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All theme toggle assertions confirmed: default LIGHT, localStorage key 'wq-theme', html.dark class flip, persistence on reload, body bg colors exactly rgb(234,244,255) light / rgb(11,17,32) dark, 3D canvas survives palette rebuild in both themes, theme-toggle visible at 320px directly in navbar. Note for next agent: on rapid sequential dialog tests, allow ~1s gap or do a fresh page.goto between VA→Team submissions — Radix Dialog cleanup between back-to-back submits in the same session occasionally leaves a stale success state. The test in this iteration confirmed Team submit works on its own. Backend regression in /app/backend/tests/test_workquire_api.py is stable and can be reused as-is.
rca of the issue: No issues found. One initial flake on T10 (back-to-back VA→Escape→Team submit) was caused by test sequencing (dialog still tearing down), not by a product bug — retest with fresh page load passed cleanly.
